skills.name: using-superpowers
skills.description: Bootstrap skill — auto-loaded at session start. Explains what skills are, how to invoke them, and what capabilities are available. Acts as the entry point for the agent's meta-cognition about its own toolset.
skills.whenToUse: Automatically loaded at the start of every session. Not invoked manually.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Structured divergent thinking before any creative work — features, components, new functionality, or system design. Guides the agent through a ideation process: explore problem space, generate options, evaluate trade-offs, select direction.
skills.whenToUse: Before designing a new feature, component, or system. Before writing plans. When facing an open-ended problem with multiple valid approaches.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.description: Converts a specification or brief into a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan. Produces a structured document with numbered tasks, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and risk notes. Intended to be followed by subagent-driven-development.
skills.whenToUse: When you have a clear spec and are about to start coding. After brainstorming and before touching any files.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Executing plans by decomposing work into independent tasks dispatched to sub-agents within the current session. Each task runs in parallel where possible. The orchestrator monitors progress, handles failures, and integrates results.
skills.whenToUse: When executing a written plan. When the plan contains tasks that can be parallelised. For any work expected to take more than 3-4 tool calls.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.description: Write failing tests before writing implementation code. Defines the TDD loop: red → green → refactor. Includes guidance on test granularity, what to test, and how to structure test files alongside implementation.
skills.whenToUse: Before writing any implementation code for a new feature or function. When adding behaviour to an existing system.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: A structured approach to diagnosing any bug, test failure, or unexpected behaviour. Steps: reproduce reliably → isolate → form hypothesis → verify → fix → confirm fix → add regression test.
skills.whenToUse: Any time a bug is encountered. When a test is failing unexpectedly. When behaviour doesn't match the spec.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.description: Checklist-driven verification pass run before claiming any task is complete. Covers: does it work end-to-end, do tests pass, is there no regressions, is the code clean, is documentation updated.
skills.whenToUse: Before marking any task as done. Before telling the user work is complete. After a fix to verify no regressions.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.description: Prepares a code review request with context for the reviewer. Summarises: what changed, why, how to test it, known trade-offs, and areas of uncertainty. Produces either a PR description or a review-request message.
skills.whenToUse: After completing a task or major feature. When the implementation involves significant trade-offs worth discussing. When handing work off to a human reviewer.

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.description: End-of-branch checklist: all tests pass, no linting errors, feature branch is rebased on main, commit messages are clean, PR description is written, and the feature is manually verified against acceptance criteria.
skills.whenToUse: Implementation is complete and all tests pass. Before opening a pull request. Before marking the branch ready for merge.

typeDescriptions.bootstrap: Loaded automatically at session start. Provides orientation and meta-capability awareness.
typeDescriptions.process: Invoked to guide the agent through a structured multi-step process before or during work.
typeDescriptions.quality: Invoked to enforce quality standards: testing, debugging, and verification.
typeDescriptions.collaboration: Invoked when work crosses boundaries — review, handoff, or PR creation.
